formula_note: Direct transfer from the official White Paper figure reported by HRW.
uncertainty_note: Solid numerator, but not paired with a source-direct Indian assault-force denominator.
quality_note: Useful standalone military death estimate, but insufficient for DR.

formula_note: Direct transfer of the official mixed 'civilians/terrorists' death figure.
uncertainty_note: This is precisely why no civilian DR is published here: the core official source merges civilians and militants.
quality_note: Retained as mixed-status mortality context only.

formula_note: Low = (950 pilgrims + 380 priests/employees/families) * 4/30. Best = add half of the 1,700 Akali supporters as status-sensitive civilian/political presence. High = count all 1,700 Akali supporters as civilian/political presence, then convert over 4 days.
uncertainty_note: The main issue is classification of the Akali supporters. A lower but cleaner bound is available even if they are excluded.
quality_note: This is the strongest small-area civilian exposure path recovered for the operation.

victim_side: Armed defenders / militants inside the complex
formula_note: Low = 500 followers of Bhindranwale * 4/30. High = (500 followers + 150 members of other armed groups) * 4/30. Best = midpoint of the two fighter counts over the same 4-day window.
uncertainty_note: This is a defender-side militant denominator only; it does not solve the missing Indian assault-force denominator.
quality_note: Useful fallback denominator path for the armed defenders, but not enough to publish DR.
